key_points: Apartment shortage is real — find one ASAP after acceptance
Limited on-campus options push students off-campus quickly
Parking has always been notoriously bad, per multiple alumni
key_quotes: Another thing is to find an apartment complex as soon as possible because they ain't too many to choose from
The parking has always sucked

key_points: Legendary party school reputation — Greek life runs hot every night
Sports culture dominates social life; basketball and football are religion
School spirit is overwhelming — nearly universal among students and alumni
key_quotes: I graduated from Tech in 2000 and was a pike and that is exactly what it was like every night, not just the weekends. Plan on failing out one year
The Partying, Mac's RIZZ, The Fights, The Frat Tour, Chips Threesome
Tech is known for the most loyal fan base in the state

key_points: On-campus food gets low marks from students
Fresh Plate in the SUB has limited hours — frustrates students with early classes
Off-campus options on University Ave praised as solid alternative
key_quotes: Food here is not great, but just go across the way and all good restaurants on University
I can't stay until 9 am, brother. I have class
Spankys and Torchys by Tech is an absolute blessing

key_points: No direct student data on counseling services in either source
Heavy party culture and academic pressure create burnout risk
One student described working 30 hours a week while taking max credits — wiped out
key_quotes: After taking thirty credits spring semester to graduate with my associate's degree at a community college while still attending Texas Tech, I guess I am just wiped out
I just ended up settling for the cheapest place with the best reputation. There's just no reason for me to be there

key_points: National Merit Scholarship offers full rides — a major draw for top students
International students report near-zero institutional aid
One family's FAFSA wasn't finalized until after their daughter started — caused major stress
key_quotes: I am 95% sure that I will be a National Merit finalist... I could get full rides at colleges like Texas Tech
My family maximum budget is around 30k a year so I won't be able to attend
The decision on her FAFSA wasn't finalized till after she started

key_points: Chancellor's censorship memo actively restricting what professors can teach
Faculty changing syllabi mid-semester; some readings removed without replacement
CS department described as subpar by a current student who transferred out
key_quotes: The CS department isn't ideal, there are many professors that aren't very good and the foundational engineering program wastes a lot of time on mostly unimportant content
One professor specifically told us that there would be readings he was leaving on the syllabus that he would not be allowed to teach in the course
Those of you who think this won't affect your education are dreaming. Faculty were told that if they mention their SPOUSE in class, they have to run that content by the Board of Regents

key_points: Chancellor Creighton not a Tech alum, doesn't live in Lubbock, seen as outsider
University paid $1.2M above market value for chancellor's new residence
Faculty Senate formally condemned chancellor's censorship policies — extremely rare
key_quotes: I know he was picked because he helped Abbott get vouchers, but the dude doesn't even have any affiliation with Tech. He doesn't care about Tech
You'd think with a $1.2 million salary the dude could buy his own house
Clearly this university doesn't care about its public image given its choice of new chancellor

key_points: Lubbock is small and isolated — arriving from Houston or Austin is a genuine shock
Campus itself is widely praised as beautiful and well-maintained
Extremely dry, flat, West Texas climate — humidifiers recommended by students
key_quotes: Living in Austin, DFW or Houston, when you arrive in Lubbock, its a shock. You can drive around the entire city in 15 mins
I was shocked to hear the girls say you need a humidifier
Tech Alum from early 90s. I haven't been on campus in almost 20 years. Living in Huntington Beach Cali now but I still keep up with my University

key_points: 92% job placement rate claimed by admissions for Rawls College of Business
Record 9 players drafted in one NFL Draft signals rising athletic prestige
Censorship policies drawing alumni concern that degrees are losing value nationally
key_quotes: 92% job placement rate in our Rawls College of Business
Texas Tech had a program record 9 players drafted in this year's NFL draft

If you have any work in a blue state during the early phase of your career when where you went to school matters, you should be really pissed about this rightward shift. Your degrees are becoming worth less

key_points: Near-free for National Merit Scholars — exceptional deal for top students
For international students with no aid, cost-benefit is questionable
Censorship policies actively threatening degree value in national job markets
key_quotes: TTU is basically free for me
This was the FIRST thing that disqualified my son from attending Tech. Had scholarships and everything but opted to go out of state
Your degrees are losing value by the minute

key_points: Intense school spirit and sports culture define the Tech identity
Political tensions are high and increasingly affect daily campus life
Alumni overwhelmingly nostalgic, but current students face a different institution
key_quotes: 2018 alum. TTU was my home and will always be. All I could think while at UT for my master's was how much I missed TTU
I went to Tech. I'm sorry to say they have become a racist breeding ground. They have become a beacon to all things Christofascist
God, I'm so embarrassed to hold a degree from this place

key_points: Academic Freedom: Chancellor banned teaching on gender, sexuality, race across all courses
Free Speech Double Standard: student expelled for mocking Charlie Kirk; swastikas went unpunished in 2011
Corruption: $1.2M overpayment on chancellor's home; existing chancellor residence already existed
key_quotes: Not only will our credibility as a respected public institution of higher education be destroyed, but you are ordering us as faculty to impose your favored viewpoints
So making fun of Charlie Kirk in the FREE SPEECH area can get you expelled from Texas Tech university?
never mind that there was ALREADY a home for the Chancellor

key_points: World-class recreation facilities — pools, gyms, library all highly rated
Honors College offers smaller class sizes and easier research placement
Incredibly tight-knit alumni network; graduates describe lifelong friendships
key_quotes: Tech has wonderful recreational facilities, parks, pools, gyms, amazing library. World class faculty
The Honors College has easier to get placement for research and get priority for class registration
98% said it's the friends they made — my experience too
